story.asA: a photographer with hundreds of Lightroom presets
story.iWant: to convert multiple preset files in parallel via the command line
story.soThat: I can efficiently migrate my entire preset library to Nikon Z format in seconds instead of minutes

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Batch Mode with Glob Patterns - Command accepts glob pattern, expands to file list, processes all matched files in parallel, writes output to same directory, exit code 0 if all succeed

Parallel Processing with Worker Pool - Uses goroutines + channels, worker pool size defaults to runtime.NumCPU(), --parallel flag allows custom worker count, each worker processes independently, results aggregated via channel
Performance Target - 100 Files in <2 Seconds - Batch conversion of 100 files completes in under 2 seconds, measured with time command, parallel processing validated, no degradation with mixed formats

Progress Indicator with Live Updates - Displays "Processing 45/100 files..." updates every 100ms or per file, final summary shows success/error counts and duration, progress to stderr not stdout, no progress in JSON mode

Error Handling - Continue on Error - --continue-on-error flag (default true) processes all files even if some fail, --fail-fast stops on first error, individual errors logged but don't stop batch, final summary shows counts, exit code 1 if any errors
Custom Output Directory - --output-dir flag specifies output directory, default is same directory as input, creates directory if missing, preserves input filename with changed extension

Batch Result Aggregation - Collects results from all workers via channel, aggregates success/error counts, tracks total duration, individual results available in JSON mode, summary includes total/success/errors/duration
Glob Pattern Validation - Validates glob pattern expands to at least 1 file, error if no matches, error if pattern invalid, supports standard glob syntax (*, ?, [abc], [a-z])
Overwrite Protection in Batch Mode - Same overwrite protection as single file mode, --overwrite flag applies to all files, skips existing files with warning if not set, summary shows skipped count

Integration with Single File Convert (Story 3-2) - Reuses format detection from format.go, reuses file I/O helpers, reuses success message formatting, maintains architectural consistency, no code duplication

doc.title: Product Requirements Document
doc.snippet: 10-100x performance improvement over Python version. Single file <20ms (vs 50-200ms), batch 100 files <2s (vs 5-20s). Cross-platform single binary distribution for Windows, macOS, Linux.

doc.title: Epic 3 Technical Specification - CLI Interface
doc.snippet: Batch processing orchestration module handles file glob patterns, parallel CPU utilization, and progress tracking. Worker pool pattern for parallel processing with goroutines + channels.

doc.title: Epic 3 Technical Specification - CLI Interface
doc.section: Workflows and Sequencing - Batch Conversion Flow
doc.snippet: Parse CLI flags → Glob pattern to file list → Create worker pool (N = runtime.NumCPU()) → Start progress tracking → Distribute files to workers → Each worker: Read, Detect, Convert, Write → Aggregate results → Display summary

doc.title: Epic 3 Technical Specification - CLI Interface
doc.snippet: Batch Processing: Convert 100 files in <2 seconds using parallel processing. Worker pool pattern with fixed-size pool of goroutines, jobs distributed via channel. Memory efficiency: <100MB for 1,000 file batch operations.

doc.title: Epic 3 Technical Specification - CLI Interface
doc.snippet: BatchResult struct contains TotalFiles, SuccessCount, ErrorCount, SkippedCount, TotalDuration, and Results array of ConversionResult. Used for batch operation aggregation and JSON output.

doc.title: Architecture Document
doc.snippet: CLI entry points in cmd/ use internal/converter as single source of truth. All business logic in internal/converter, CLI only handles I/O and formatting. Thin CLI layer pattern with stateless operations.

doc.title: Architecture Document
doc.snippet: Go goroutines and channels provide efficient parallel processing. Worker pool pattern: fixed-size pool of goroutines processing jobs from channel. Thread-safe stateless converter enables concurrent conversions without synchronization.

doc.title: Story 3.2 - Convert Command Implementation
doc.snippet: Format detection: detectFormat() and validateFormat() from cmd/cli/format.go. File I/O helpers: generateOutputPath(), checkOutputExists(), ensureOutputDir(). Output formatting: formatBytes(), formatDuration() for human-readable display.

artifact.reason: Core conversion API that batch processing will call for each file. Thread-safe stateless function enabling parallel execution. Performs validate → parse → generate flow.
Auto-detects format from file content (magic bytes, XML structure, Lua syntax). Will be reused by batch processing for format detection before conversion.
Structured error type for conversion failures. Batch processing needs to collect and aggregate these errors for summary reporting.
Universal intermediate representation used by all conversions. Understanding this model helps with debugging conversion results in batch operations.

go.package: Cobra CLI framework - provides command structure, flag parsing, help generation
Used for runtime.NumCPU() to determine default worker pool size
WaitGroup and atomic operations for worker pool synchronization
Atomic counter for thread-safe progress tracking
Glob pattern expansion (filepath.Glob) and cross-platform path handling
Duration tracking for batch timing and performance measurement
JSON marshaling for --json output mode (BatchResult struct)

constraints.constraint: Thin CLI Layer - All business logic stays in internal/converter, CLI only handles I/O, flag parsing, and output formatting
No Global State - Worker pool uses channels for coordination, no shared mutable state between goroutines
Error Wrapping - Preserve underlying errors with ConversionError context for debugging
Overwrite Protection - Same pattern as single file mode (Story 3-2), requires --overwrite flag to replace existing files
Reuse Story 3-2 Helpers - MUST reuse format detection, file I/O helpers, and output formatting from Story 3-2 to avoid code duplication
Thread Safety - All converter calls must be thread-safe and stateless to enable concurrent batch processing
Performance Target - 100 files in <2 seconds on modern hardware (validation required via benchmarks)
Progress to Stderr - Progress indicators output to stderr, final results to stdout (separation for scripting)
Exit Codes - 0=success, 1=error (unless --continue-on-error allows completion with errors)

interface.name: converter.Convert
interface.signature: func Convert(input []byte, from, to string) ([]byte, error)
interface.usage: Called by each worker goroutine for individual file conversions in batch mode

interface.name: filepath.Glob
interface.usage: Expands glob pattern (e.g., "*.xmp") to list of matching file paths for batch processing

interface.name: runtime.NumCPU
interface.usage: Determines default worker pool size - number of CPU cores available for parallel processing

interface.name: BatchFlags
interface.signature: type BatchFlags struct { To string; OutputDir string; Parallel int; Verbose bool; JSON bool; ContinueOnError bool; Overwrite bool }
interface.usage: Flag structure for batch command options, passed to batch processing orchestration

interface.name: BatchResult
interface.signature: type BatchResult struct { TotalFiles int; SuccessCount int; ErrorCount int; SkippedCount int; TotalDuration time.Duration; Results []ConversionResult }
interface.usage: Aggregates results from all workers for final summary and JSON output

interface.name: ConversionResult
interface.usage: Individual file conversion result, used in both single and batch modes

tests.standards: Epic 3 testing follows standard Go testing patterns with table-driven tests. Unit tests for worker pool logic and aggregation functions. Integration tests execute full CLI with real sample files from testdata/. Performance benchmarks validate <2s target for 100 files. Cross-platform tests ensure consistent behavior on Windows/macOS/Linux. Manual tests verify progress indicator UX and error message clarity.

locations.location: cmd/cli/batch_test.go - Unit tests for worker pool, aggregation logic, flag parsing
cmd/cli/batch_integration_test.go - End-to-end CLI execution with 100 real sample files
testdata/xmp/ - 913 sample XMP files for validation
testdata/np3/ - 22 sample NP3 files for validation
testdata/lrtemplate/ - 544 sample lrtemplate files for validation

ideas.idea: Test glob pattern expansion with wildcards (*.xmp, *.{xmp,np3}, **/*.xmp)
Test worker pool with different sizes (1, 4, 8, runtime.NumCPU())
Benchmark 100 files with time command, verify <2s target, compare sequential vs parallel
Test progress indicator updates correctly (count increments, final summary displays)
Test continue-on-error with mixed valid/invalid files, verify all attempted
Test fail-fast mode stops on first error, verify remaining files not processed
Test output-dir creates missing directories, preserves input filenames
Test result aggregation matches individual results, JSON output has all fields
Test glob validation with no matches returns error, invalid pattern returns error
Test overwrite protection in batch (skip existing without --overwrite, replace with flag)
Verify no code duplication from Story 3-2 (format detection, I/O helpers reused)
